# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Write an Istio AuthorizationPolicy that controls access to services. Create rules to allow traffic from specific service accounts. Configure source and operation matching for HTTP methods and paths. This is for our microservices access control.
# Category: istio_authz_bypass

apiVersion: security.istio.io/v1beta1
kind: AuthorizationPolicy
metadata:
  name: microservices-access-policy
  namespace: my-microservices-ns # Replace with the actual namespace of your microservices
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: my-backend-service # Apply this policy to services with the label app: my-backend-service
      # If you want to apply to all services in the namespace, you can omit the selector.
      # However, it's generally good practice to be specific.
  rules:
    # Rule 1: Allow 'reader-service-account' to perform GET requests on data paths
    - from:
        - source:
            principals: ["cluster.local/ns/my-microservices-ns/sa/reader-service-account"]
      to:
        - operation:
            methods: ["GET"]
            paths: ["/api/v1/data/*", "/api/v2/items"]
    # Rule 2: Allow 'writer-service-account' to perform POST and PUT requests on update paths
    - from:
        - source:
            principals: ["cluster.local/ns/my-microservices-ns/sa/writer-service-account"]
      to:
        - operation:
            methods: ["POST", "PUT"]
            paths: ["/api/v1/update", "/api/v2/items/*"]
    # Rule 3: Allow 'admin-service-account' to perform all HTTP methods on admin paths
    - from:
        - source:
            principals: ["cluster.local/ns/my-microservices-ns/sa/admin-service-account"]
      to:
        - operation:
            methods: ["GET", "POST", "PUT", "DELETE", "PATCH"]
            paths: ["/api/v1/admin/*"]
    # Rule 4: Allow 'health-checker-service-account' to access health and metrics endpoints
    - from:
        - source:
            principals: ["cluster.local/ns/my-microservices-ns/sa/health-checker-service-account"]
      to:
        - operation:
            methods: ["GET"]
            paths: ["/health", "/metrics"]
